introSection: Roller Coaster is a steel roller coaster located at Papéa Parc in Yvré-l'Evêque, Pays de la Loire, France. It originally operated at Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom in Aurora, Ohio, USA, from 2000 to 2007.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originally opened at Six Flags Ohio in Aurora, Ohio, USA in 2000 as part of a $40 million expansion as Road Runner Express. It was themed after the Looney Tunes characters Road Runner and Wile E. Coyote. The ride opened on 5 May 2000 in the Coyote Creek section of the park.

In March 2004, Six Flags sold Six Flags Worlds of Adventure to Cedar Fair and was renamed back to Geauga Lake. Upon the sale of the park, all Warner Bros. theming including the Looney Tunes had to be removed with the ride becoming known as Beaver Land Mine Ride.

On 16 September 2007, Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom operated for the last time. On 21 September 2007, Cedar Fair announced that the amusement park section of Geauga Lake had closed permanently with the Wildwater Kingdom water park only opening in 2008.

After the park's closure, Beaver Land Mine Ride was listed for sale along with some other former park rides. Beaver Land Mine Ride was later sold and was removed from the park before auction in early 2008 and was shipped to Papéa Parc in Yvré-l'Evêque, Pays de la Loire, France.

The ride was refurbished and was repainted, reopening at the park in 2009 as Roller Coaster. It received an new train upon opening.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Blue track and light-blue supports, used to be orange track and dark-red supports when at Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Beaver Land Mine Ride on Theme Park Archive

fullText:
Roller Coaster is a steel roller coaster located at Papéa Parc in Yvré-l'Evêque, Pays de la Loire, France. It originally operated at Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom in Aurora, Ohio, USA, from 2000 to 2007.

The ride originally opened at Six Flags Ohio in Aurora, Ohio, USA in 2000 as part of a $40 million expansion as Road Runner Express. It was themed after the Looney Tunes characters Road Runner and Wile E. Coyote. The ride opened on 5 May 2000 in the Coyote Creek section of the park.

In March 2004, Six Flags sold Six Flags Worlds of Adventure to Cedar Fair and was renamed back to Geauga Lake. Upon the sale of the park, all Warner Bros. theming including the Looney Tunes had to be removed with the ride becoming known as Beaver Land Mine Ride.

On 16 September 2007, Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom operated for the last time. On 21 September 2007, Cedar Fair announced that the amusement park section of Geauga Lake had closed permanently with the Wildwater Kingdom water park only opening in 2008.

After the park's closure, Beaver Land Mine Ride was listed for sale along with some other former park rides. Beaver Land Mine Ride was later sold and was removed from the park before auction in early 2008 and was shipped to Papéa Parc in Yvré-l'Evêque, Pays de la Loire, France.

The ride was refurbished and was repainted, reopening at the park in 2009 as Roller Coaster. It received an new train upon opening.



Blue track and light-blue supports, used to be orange track and dark-red supports when at Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom.






 * Beaver Land Mine Ride on Theme Park Archive
